ddls:
CREATE TABLE "passengers" (
  "passenger_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each passenger */,
  "survived" INTEGER /* Survival status of the passenger (0 = No, 1 = Yes) */,
  "pclass" INTEGER /* Ticket class (1 = 1st, 2 = 2nd, 3 = 3rd) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the passenger */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Gender of the passenger (male, female) */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the passenger */,
  "sibsp" INTEGER /* Number of siblings/spouses aboard */,
  "parch" INTEGER /* Number of parents/children aboard */,
  "ticket" TEXT /* Ticket number */,
  "fare" REAL /* Passenger fare */,
  "cabin" TEXT /* Cabin number */,
  "embarked" TEXT /* Port of Embarkation (C = Cherbourg, Q = Queenstown, S = Southampton) */,
  "sex_num" INTEGER /* Numerical representation of gender (0 = female, 1 = male) */,
  "name_length" INTEGER /* Length of the passenger's name */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the passenger */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the passenger */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the passenger */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education of the passenger */,
  "income" REAL /* Annual income of the passenger */,
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the family */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information for the passenger */,
  "passenger_type" TEXT /* Type of passenger (e.g., crew, passenger) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("passenger_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_passengers_pclass FOREIGN KEY ("pclass") REFERENCES travel_classes ("class_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_passengers_ticket FOREIGN KEY ("ticket") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_number"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_passengers_family_id FOREIGN KEY ("family_id") REFERENCES families ("family_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_passengers_embarked FOREIGN KEY ("embarked") REFERENCES ports ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "travel_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each travel class */,
  "class_description" TEXT /* Description of the travel class (e.g., 1st Class, 2nd Class, 3rd Class) */,
  "class_capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the travel class */,
  "class_features" TEXT /* Features of the travel class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each port (C = Cherbourg, Q = Queenstown, S = Southampton) */,
  "port_name" TEXT /* Name of the port */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the port */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the port city */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the port city */,
  "climate" TEXT /* Climate of the port city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tickets" (
  "ticket_number" TEXT /* Unique ticket number */,
  "fare" REAL /* Fare paid for the ticket */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Travel class of the ticket */,
  "cabin" TEXT /* Cabin number assigned to the ticket */,
  "booking_date" TEXT /* Date the ticket was booked */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment used for the ticket */,
  "booking_agent" TEXT /* Name of the booking agent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_number")
)

CREATE TABLE "families" (
  "family_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each family */,
  "sibsp" INTEGER /* Number of siblings/spouses aboard */,
  "parch" INTEGER /* Number of parents/children aboard */,
  "family_size" INTEGER /* Total size of the family */,
  "family_members" TEXT /* List of family members (e.g., 'John Doe, Jane Doe') */,
  "family_head" TEXT /* Name of the head of the family */,
  "family_structure" TEXT /* Brief description of the family structure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("family_id")
)
column_names: Survival status of the passenger (0 = No, 1 = Yes)
Port of Embarkation (C = Cherbourg, Q = Queenstown, S = Southampton)
Numerical representation of gender (0 = female, 1 = male)
Highest level of education of the passenger
Emergency contact information for the passenger
Unique identifier for each travel class
Description of the travel class (e.g., 1st Class, 2nd Class, 3rd Class)
Unique identifier for each port (C = Cherbourg, Q = Queenstown, S = Southampton)
Cabin number assigned to the ticket
Method of payment used for the ticket
List of family members (e.g., 'John Doe, Jane Doe')
Name of the head of the family
Brief description of the family structure
